author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
What Pelagius Thinks is Needful for Ease of Performance is Really Necessary for the Performance.
title: What Pelagius Thinks is Needful for Ease of Performance is Really Necessary for the Performance.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 30 [XXIX.]—What Pelagius Thinks is Needful for Ease of Performance is Really Necessary for the Performance.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: In like manner, in another passage of the same book, he says: “In order that men may more easily accomplish by grace that which they are commanded to do by free will.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Now, expunge the phrase “more easily,” and you leave not only a full, but also a sound sense, if it be regarded as meaning simply this: “That men may accomplish through grace what they are commanded to do by free will.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The addition of the words “more easily,” however, tacitly suggests the possibility of accomplishing good works even without the grace of God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: But such a meaning is disallowed by Him who says, “Without me ye can do nothing.”
